ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a closest parent. No specific code exists for a double-patty fast food burger meal set including fries and drink.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (Hamburger) is the closest parent. The full meal set (double patty burger + fries + drink) is not individually coded in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for a branded fast food double-patty burger meal set.

serving.description: 1 meal set (double patty burger + medium fries + medium drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One Classic Jolliburger Meal with Double Patties typically consists of a double beef patty burger, a medium serving of french fries, and a medium soft drink, totalling approximately 650 g per meal set.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Hamburger, double patty with condiments and vegetables (Fast Food); supplemented with USDA SR Legacy french fries and soft drink components for meal estimation
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 170720 (Fast food, hamburger, double patty, with condiments and vegetables) as the closest approved match for the burger component. Meal-level per-100g values are estimated by weighting burger (~300g), fries (~115g), and a medium cola (~235g) components proportionally. This is a composite meal estimation. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Hamburger bun / fast food burger composite estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Classic Jolliburger Meal with Double Patties. GI of 66 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white hamburger bun / fast food burger (GI ~66, medium category). The meal GI is dominated by the refined-flour bun and french fries components. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 152.75) / 100 ≈ 101 g GL, where carbohydrate per 650g serving = 23.5 × 6.5 = 152.75g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~2015 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal as a single 650g serving provides approximately 2015 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg and significantly raising cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load and refined carbohydrate content from bun and fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal as the combination of a refined-flour bun, french fries, and a sugary soft drink produces a high glycemic load (~101 per serving), likely causing significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and high caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this meal due to its saturated fat (~18.9g per serving), trans fat (~1.3g per serving), and high sodium content, all of which are associated with increased LDL cholesterol and arterial inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Classic Jolliburger Meal with Double Beef Patties is a fast food burger meal served by Jollibee, a Filipino-origin quick-service restaurant chain with outlets in Singapore including Bugis Village. The meal consists of a double beef patty burger — the Jolliburger — served in a soft white sesame-topped bun with lettuce, tomato, onion, ketchup, and mustard, accompanied by a medium serving of salted french fries and a medium soft drink. The beef patties are seasoned and grilled, giving the burger a savoury, slightly smoky flavour with a juicy texture. The bun is soft and slightly sweet in the Jollibee house style. The meal is certified Halal and is popular among Singaporean consumers seeking affordable fast food. Nutritionally, the meal is energy-dense, providing approximately 1287 kcal per full meal set, with significant contributions from refined carbohydrates (bun and fries), saturated fat (beef patties), and sodium (seasoning, condiments, fries). The high glycemic load (~101 per serving) makes it a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and is not recommended for frequent consumption in clinical dietary guidance contexts.

Jollibee double patty burger nutrition facts

Fast food burger meal nutrition Singapore

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional data for the Classic Jolliburger Meal with Double Patties from Jollibee Singapore is available in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Food, or equivalent). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 170720 (fast food double patty hamburger with condiments and vegetables) and weighted with fries and soft drink components. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest analogous food (white hamburger bun / fast food burger). Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite meal estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 170720 for the burger component, supplemented with USDA SR Legacy data for fries and cola). No exact Jollibee Singapore nutritional panel was available in any approved source at time of processing. 2. Serving size of 650g is an estimate based on typical fast food double-patty burger meal composition (burger ~300g, fries ~115g, medium cola ~235g). 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this food category. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this food category. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this food category. 4. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (white hamburger bun / fast food burger category); no direct GI measurement for the Jolliburger exists in the approved GI database. 5. The meal is tagged Halal based on Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification as listed on field survey data cuisine tags; this should be verified against MUIS certification records for production use. 6. Trans fat value of 0.2g per 100g is based on USDA data for the burger component; actual trans fat may vary by supplier and preparation batch.

Wrote natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context.
